mechanism_narrative: A persistently high unemployment rate indicates underlying economic weakness and reduced consumer confidence, which translates into lower corporate earnings expectations and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:High unemployment typically correlates with market downturns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 consistently generates positive 20-day returns during periods of high UNRATE (above 7%) over any 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in retail sales indicates robust consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon as investors price in improved earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong retail sales often precede positive market movements.
failure_condition: If, when RSAFS 3-month annualized growth exceeds 5%, the S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 5-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is low but inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising, it signals that monetary policy is behind the curve, leading to expectations of future aggressive tightening and increased uncertainty, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Historically, periods of low rates with rising inflation have led to market volatility.
failure_condition: If, during periods where FEDFUNDS is below 1% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 3%, the S&P 500 consistently generates positive 60-day returns, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery following a recession, a rebound in GDP growth signals improving corporate fundamentals and investor confidence, leading to strong positive S&P 500 forward returns as the market anticipates sustained growth.
evidence_refs: fact:Equity markets often rally strongly in early stages of economic recovery.
failure_condition: If, specifically during periods identified as post-recession recovery, positive GDPC1 growth does not correlate with positive S&P 500 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.05) over a 10-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts indicates strong underlying economic demand and investment, signaling future economic activity and consumer confidence, which typically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: If HOUST values consistently above 700 (thousands of units) are followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 40% of occurrences over a 7-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, reflects a healthy labor market and strong consumer purchasing power, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Robust job growth is a key driver of economic expansion and market optimism.
failure_condition: If, when PAYEMS 3-month average change exceeds 100k, the S&P 500 fails to produce positive 20-day returns in the majority of instances over any 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A decrease in the Federal Funds Rate signals an easing monetary policy stance by the central bank, aiming to stimulate economic activity. This typically lowers borrowing costs and boosts corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Rate cuts are generally seen as positive for equity markets in the short term.
failure_condition: If a decrease in FEDFUNDS by more than 0.1% in a month is followed by negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 60% of cases over a 5-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in industrial production indicates a contraction in the manufacturing and mining sectors, signaling broader economic weakness and reduced corporate output, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Declining industrial production is a classic recessionary signal.
failure_condition: If INDPRO 3-month annualized growth below -2% is consistently followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns over any 7-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.
